records.ety: [L. epodos, Gr. <?/, fr. <?/, adj., singing to, sung or said after, fr. <?/ to sing to; 'epi` upon, to + <?/ to sing: cf. F. pode. See Ode.]

defs: The after song; the part of a lyric ode which follows the strophe and antistrophe, -- the ancient ode being divided into strophe, antistrophe, and epode.
A species of lyric poem, invented by Archilochus, in which a longer verse is followed by a shorter one; as, the Epodes of Horace. It does not include the elegiac distich.
